message:
fix(security): panic on CSPRNG failure instead of silently using zeroed bytes

generate_registration_token() was ignoring the getrandom::getrandom()
return value with `let _ = ...`. If the CSPRNG failed (rare, but possible
on systems without initialized entropy), the token would be all-zeros —
a predictable, guessable value that defeats the entire sender verification
mechanism.

Now panics with a clear message. CSPRNG failure is unrecoverable for
security-sensitive code; silently degrading to zeros is worse than crashing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
